pattern: Grouping equal-letter runs lets a single swap either extend one run by an adjacent same-letter block across a one-gap, capped by how many copies of that letter exist overall.
hints: Think in runs of identical letters; a swap can borrow at most one extra copy of a letter from elsewhere.
A run can grow by one if a copy of its letter exists somewhere outside it; two runs of the same letter separated by exactly one different character can merge if a spare copy exists.

Compress the string into (char, length) runs, track total counts per character, and for each run consider extending it and bridging a single-character gap to the next same-letter run, bounded by the available count.

edge_inputs.description: bridge two runs across a gap
already all same, no swap needed
only one copy of the letter cannot grow
gap with no spare copy elsewhere
long run plus a far spare

stress.description: random small-alphabet strings for brute comparison
stress.note: length capped because the swap oracle is O(n^3)

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maxRepOpt1(self, text):
        from collections import Counter
        total = Counter(text)
        runs = []
        i = 0
        n = len(text)
        while i < n:
            j = i
            while j < n and text[j] == text[i]:
                j += 1
            runs.append((text[i], j - i))
            i = j
        best = 0
        for idx, (ch, length) in enumerate(runs):
            # extend this run by one if a spare copy exists
            cand = min(length + 1, total[ch])
            best = max(best, cand)
            # bridge to next same-letter run across a single-char gap
            if idx + 2 < len(runs) and runs[idx + 1][1] == 1 and runs[idx + 2][0] == ch:
                merged = length + runs[idx + 2][1]
                cand = min(merged + 1, total[ch])
                best = max(best, cand)
        return best
